abstract: While some might suggest that these two agendas are complementary—with decolonial critiques opening political space for decentring strategies to implement pragmatic, incremental change—there is no assurance that such synergy will occur. This is particularly true if the distinct goals of decentring and decoloniality continue to be blurred. While a decentring approach might improve the perceived legitimacy and efficiency of EU foreign policy, it risks leaving the underlying "coloniality of power" untouched or even further entrenched. Consequently, rather than simply attempting to "decentre Europe," we must shift toward decolonial futures that center the democratic struggle against persistent colonial structures and hierarchies.
